newsSince: Fuji Electric continues to build out DC-relevant power capacity: a new Malaysia factory building with construction starting June 2026 (operation Oct 2026), Tsukuba switchboard/power-supply capacity expansion to ~1.7x (construction Sept 2026), Kobe expansion to ~1.5x (Oct 2026), and SiC lines ramping in FY2026 with module sales targeted to more than double. Most plans were disclosed pre-June 6, but the Malaysia construction start is a fresh execution milestone.

sources.label: Fuji Electric expands overseas switchboard capacity for DC business
Fuji Electric Tsukuba switchboard/power-supply capacity expansion
Fuji Electric 3D-wiring SiC module, ~50% volume/loss reduction (MarketScreener)
Fuji Electric ejector cooling for data centers & FY26 strategy (note.com)
Fuji Electric: Malaysia switchboard capacity expansion

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: power-semis + DC switchboard/power-supply capacity expansions (Malaysia, Tsukuba 1.7x, Kobe 1.5x) and SiC doubling are real bottleneck adjacency, but all predate June 9 and are already reflected. Bucketed 'secondary' - genuine adjacency, only modest mispricing. Hold rank 37.
verdicts.bull: DC switchboard capacity expansions plus SiC module sales doubling tie directly to AI power-delivery demand; multiple capacity adds coming online late 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Real bottleneck adjacency but not as mispriced as top residual-alpha names; catalysts already disclosed.
verdicts.bear: Power-systems story increasingly understood; modest residual alpha; execution/ramp risk on new capacity.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: DC switchboard/SiC capacity ramps reaching full operation in 2H26
verdicts.primaryRisk: Ramp execution; modest mispricing leaves limited alpha
